en.extract:
The puff-throated bulbul (Alophoixus pallidus) is a songbird in the bulbul family, Pycnonotidae. The species was first described by Robert Swinhoe in 1870. It is found in Southeast Asia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests.


Taxonomy and systematics
The puff-throated bulbul was originally described in the genus Criniger until moved to the genus Alophoixus in 2009. Alternate names for the puff-throated bulbul include the olivaceous bearded-bulbul, olivaceous bulbul and white-throated bulbul. The latter name should not be confused with the species of the same name, Alophoixus flaveolus.


Subspecies
Seven subspecies are recognized:

Grey-crowned bulbul (A. p. griseiceps) - (Hume, 1873): Originally described as a separate species in the genus Criniger. Found in the Pegu Yoma Mountains (south-central Myanmar)
A. p. robinsoni - (Ticehurst, 1932): Found in Tenasserim (south-eastern Myanmar)
A. p. henrici - (Oustalet, 1896): Originally described as a separate species in the genus Criniger. Found in south-western China, eastern Myanmar, northern Thailand and northern Indochina
A. p. pallidus - (R. Swinhoe, 1870): Found on Hainan Island (off south-eastern China)
A. p. isani - (Deignan, 1956): Found in north-eastern Thailand
A. p. annamensis - (Delacour & Jabouille, 1924): Found in central Indochina
A. p. khmerensis - (Deignan, 1956): Found in southern Indochina


Description
The puff-throated bulbul is a large (23 cm long) bulbul with olive upperparts, yellow underparts, and puffy white throat feathers. It is similar in appearance to the white-throated bulbul but with duller underparts. It has black feet and brown iris and bill. Males and females are visually indistinguishable.


Behaviour and ecology
The puff-throated bulbul is a regular but not an obligate cooperative breeder. Groups can comprise one or more breeding pairs and breed either cooperatively or non-cooperatively. In cases of multiple breeding pairs in a single territory, the pairs nest separately. The female builds the nest and incubates and broods the young. Males and helpers aid in feeding the nestlings and fledglings. Nests are open cups typically located in the understorey.


References

The '''puff-throated bulbul''' ('''''Alophoixus pallidus''''') is a [[songbird]] in the [[bulbul]] family, Pycnonotidae. The species was [[Species description|first described]] by [[Robert Swinhoe]] in 1870. It is found in Southeast Asia. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
The puff-throated bulbul was originally described in the genus ''[[Criniger]]'' until moved to the genus ''Alophoixus'' in 2009.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/updates/archives/taxonomy-version-2/|title=Taxonomy Version 2 « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-05-12}}</ref> Alternate names for the puff-throated bulbul include the '''olivaceous bearded-bulbul''', '''olivaceous bulbul''' and '''white-throated bulbul'''. The latter name should not be confused with the species of the same name, ''[[Alophoixus flaveolus]]''.

===Subspecies===
Seven subspecies are recognized:<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/bulbuls/|title=Bulbuls « IOC World Bird List|website=www.worldbirdnames.org|language=en-US|access-date=2017-05-14}}</ref>
* '''Grey-crowned bulbul''' (''A. p. griseiceps'') - <small>([[Allan Octavian Hume|Hume]], 1873)</small>: Originally described as a separate species in the genus ''Criniger''. Found in the [[Pegu Yoma |Pegu Yoma Mountains]] (south-central Myanmar)	
* ''A. p. robinsoni'' - <small>([[Claud B. Ticehurst|Ticehurst]], 1932)</small>: Found in [[Tanintharyi Region|Tenasserim]] (south-eastern Myanmar)
* ''A. p. henrici'' - <small>([[Émile Oustalet|Oustalet]], 1896)</small>: Originally described as a separate species in the genus ''Criniger''. Found in south-western China, eastern Myanmar, northern Thailand and northern Indochina
* ''A. p. pallidus'' - <small>([[Robert Swinhoe|R. Swinhoe]], 1870)</small>: Found on [[Hainan Island]] (off south-eastern China)
* ''A. p. isani'' - <small>([[Herbert Girton Deignan|Deignan]], 1956)</small>: Found in north-eastern Thailand
* ''A. p. annamensis'' - <small>([[Jean Théodore Delacour|Delacour]] & [[Pierre Jabouille|Jabouille]], 1924)</small>: Found in central [[Indochina]]
* ''A. p. khmerensis'' - <small>(Deignan, 1956)</small>: Found in southern Indochina

==Description==
The puff-throated bulbul is a large (23 cm long) bulbul with olive upperparts, yellow underparts, and puffy white throat feathers. It is similar in appearance to the [[white-throated bulbul]] but with duller underparts. It has black feet and brown iris and bill. Males and females are visually indistinguishable.<ref>{{cite book|last=MacKinnon|first=John|title=A Field Guide to the Birds of China|year=2000|publisher=Oxford University Press|location=USA|isbn=0-19-854940-7|author2=Karen Phillipps|url-access=registration|url=https://archive.org/details/fieldguidetobird00john}}</ref>

==Behaviour and ecology==
The puff-throated bulbul is a regular but not an obligate [[Cooperative breeding|cooperative breeder]]. Groups can comprise one or more breeding pairs and breed either cooperatively or non-cooperatively. In cases of multiple breeding pairs in a single territory, the pairs nest separately. The female builds the nest and incubates and broods the young. Males and [[Cooperative breeding#Helpers|helpers]] aid in feeding the nestlings and fledglings. Nests are open cups typically located in the understorey.<ref>{{cite journal|last=Pierce|first=A.J.|author2=K. Tokue |author3=K. Pobprasert |author4= W. Sankamethawee |title=Cooperative breeding in the Puff-throated Bulbul ''Alophoixus pallidus'' in Thailand|journal=Raffles Bulletin of Zoology|year=2007|volume=55|pages=199–201}}</ref>

zh.extract:
白喉冠鹎（学名：Alophoixus pallidus）为鹎科冠鹎属的鸟类。分布于中南半岛以及中国大陆的云南、贵州、广西、海南等地，常见于海拔1500米以下的山坡、山谷、溪畔的开阔次生阔叶林、季雨林、雨林或平坝散生树丛中。该物种的模式产地在海南岛。


亚种
白喉冠鹎西南亚种（学名：Alophoixus pallidus henrici）。分布于缅甸、老挝、泰国、越南以及中国大陆的云南、贵州、广西等地。该物种的模式产地在云南和越南北部。
白喉冠鹎指名亚种（学名：Alophoixus pallidus pallidus），是中国的特有物种。分布于海南等地。该物种的模式产地在海南岛。
